task_completion.description: Set the observation location to Los Angeles, California (34.05°N, 118.24°W)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium location window or the bottom status bar displays 'Los Angeles' or coordinates approximately 34°N and 118°W.

task_completion.description: Set the date and time to November 30, 2023, at 20:00 UTC
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium date/time window or bottom status bar shows the date '2023-11-30' and the time corresponding to 20:00 UTC (or local equivalent, e.g., 12:00 if timezone is UTC-8).

task_completion.description: Disable the atmosphere to make stars and constellations visible during the daytime
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sky background is dark/black (space) despite the Sun being visible, and the atmosphere toggle button in the bottom toolbar is deactivated.

task_completion.description: Enable the ecliptic line, constellation boundaries, and constellation names
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line representing the ecliptic is visible crossing the sky, constellation boundaries (typically red dashed lines) are shown, and constellation names (like 'Ophiuchus') are displayed.

task_completion.description: Demonstrate the Sun positioned within the Ophiuchus boundaries
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sun is clearly visible on the screen and is located inside the boundary lines of the Ophiuchus constellation.

task_completion.description: Write a video script outline mentioning the date and required keywords
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the file 'ophiuchus_script.txt' containing the date 'November 30, 2023' and the keywords 'Sun', 'Ophiuchus', and 'Ecliptic'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Stellarium application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine Stellarium GUI with its standard toolbars, menus, and rendering engine, not a web browser showing an image or a different planetarium software.

integrity.description: The agent actually created and edited the script file in the environment
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, mousepad) or terminal commands (like echo/cat) are visible showing the creation of the script at ~/Desktop/ophiuchus_script.txt.

integrity.description: The screenshot of the Sun in Ophiuchus is a genuine render from the software
integrity.visual_evidence: The astronomical render matches the UI state (e.g., time, location, toggles) without signs of text being artificially overlaid or images being pasted from external sources.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Location: Los Angeles (34.05 N, -118.24 E). Target Date/Time: 2023-11-30 at 20:00:00 UTC. Script path: /home/ga/Desktop/ophiuchus_script.txt. Screenshots directory: /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium. The VLM should verify the Sun is within the boundaries of Ophiuchus with the atmosphere turned off (dark sky) and the ecliptic line visible.
